<!--
Thanks for contributing to Virtual Frame!

PR title must follow Conventional Commits — e.g. `feat(react): add useFrame hook`,
`fix(core): handle null bridge target`. The `pr-title` CI check enforces this.

If your PR changes any package code, add a changeset:
    pnpm changeset
…and commit the generated `.changeset/*.md` file alongside your change.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences on what this PR does. -->

## Motivation

<!--
Why is this change needed? Link the issue or discussion it addresses.
Closes #
-->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behavior)
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Build / CI / repo tooling
- [ ] Test-only change

## Test plan

<!--
How did you verify this works? Include commands you ran, screenshots,
recordings, or links to passing CI runs. PRs without a test plan will
be sent back.
-->

## Screenshots / recordings

<!-- For UI changes. Delete this section otherwise. -->

## Breaking changes

<!--
List anything an existing user must do to upgrade. Delete this section
if there are no breaking changes.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] Title follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).
- [ ] I added a changeset (`pnpm changeset`) — or this PR doesn't ship code (in which case I applied the `skip-changeset` label).
- [ ] Tests cover the new behavior (or I explained why none are needed).
- [ ] Documentation in `docs/` was updated for any user-visible change.
